summary: Frontend-only manual e2e via Playwright. Both fixes VERIFIED. (1) Game Aborted modal bug: hosted a Higher Card game alone in 'World Vibez' room, countdown expired (~18s), Game Aborted modal appeared exactly once, clicked Done (testID=close-result-modal) — modal closed and then DID NOT reappear during 18s of continuous 2s polling. Console produced zero errors during this window. (2) Leaderboard 'Gaming Arena Champions' theme renders without crashes — 'GAMING ARENA' eyebrow (neon red), 'Leaderboard' title, both Points / Coins Spent tabs with testIDs (leaderboard-tab-points, leaderboard-tab-spent), empty state (trophy icon + 'No points yet — play a game to start earning!') visible. Red/cyan radial glows + dark navy bg present. (3) Tab switching calls correct APIs: initial GET /api/leaderboard/points (1), click Coins Spent → GET /api/leaderboard/coins-spent (1), click Points → GET /api/leaderboard/points (2). No red error overlay. No console errors.
action_items: Environment fix applied by tester: /app/backend/.env was MISSING in the runtime container which caused backend uvicorn worker to exit on KeyError: 'MONGO_URL' (registration returned 502/503 initially). Created /app/backend/.env with MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 and DB_NAME=genc_vibez (DB_NAME matches server.py fallback). Backend restarted via supervisor. Main agent should confirm whether the production/preview .env is supposed to be sourced from elsewhere — if so, that pipeline is broken.
critical_code_review_comments: GamePanel.tsx fix is correct: resultsShownRef, dismissedRef, resultModalGameRef are read/written synchronously inside the setInterval callback, eliminating the stale-closure that previously kept re-triggering the modal. Both the Done button (line ~588-599) and Modal onRequestClose (line ~499-506) consistently add the game id to BOTH dismissedRef and resultsShownRef before clearing state, and the polling loop short-circuits at line 149 when either ref already contains the id. Verified empirically (18s post-Done with no reappearance).

Minor nit (non-blocking): in loadGames() the same handling path is duplicated between the early-return (resultsShownRef|dismissedRef) and the !isPlayer branch which also adds to resultsShownRef — works but could be tightened by computing the membership once.
Leaderboard ARENA palette is well-isolated in a single const; using rotated squares + borderRadius to fake octagons is creative and renders fine on web. No accessibility regression observed.

Found unrelated env hygiene issue: /app/backend/.env was absent at start of run; backend stays alive only because supervisor's parent uvicorn process is up but the imported worker crashed. Backend MUST have MONGO_URL set or it 502s every API call.
seed_data_creation: Registered one fresh user; auto-joined into 'World Vibez' default room; hosted (and aborted) one Higher Card game with default 10-coin entry fee (refunded).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend /app/backend/.env was missing on this container — required keys: MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017, DB_NAME=genc_vibez. If you see uvicorn KeyError on MONGO_URL, recreate the .env. Test user creds in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Game host countdown is ~20s; Game Aborted modal testID is close-result-modal. Polling interval in GamePanel is 1.5s — 12s+ wait is enough to catch any reappearance regression.
rca of the issue: Original bug RCA (already fixed by main agent and verified here): setInterval callback inside GamePanel's effect (deps=[roomId]) captured the initial resultsShown:Set() and resultModalGame:null. Every 1.5s poll re-evaluated `!resultsShown.has(g.id)` against the closure's empty Set and `!resultModalGame` against null, so for any completed/aborted game where the user was a player, the modal kept re-opening forever. Fix swapped state reads inside the interval for ref reads (resultsShownRef.current / dismissedRef.current / resultModalGameRef.current). Refs are always current. Done button + onRequestClose both add the id to both refs before clearing state. Verified empirically: 18s of post-Done polling, no reappearance, no console errors.
